mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which tends to lower long-term interest rates as investors anticipate sustained low borrowing costs and seek higher yields in other assets, driving the 10-year Treasury yield down.
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic slowdown, leading investors to seek safe-haven assets like Treasuries, thus increasing demand and driving the 10-year Treasury yield down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:flight to safety during economic downturns
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with robust industrial production indicates an overheating economy, increasing inflation expectations and the likelihood of monetary tightening, which drives long-term interest rates, including the 10-year Treasury yield, higher.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 3% annualized AND INDPRO growth > 0.5% MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 50% of periods where both GDPC1 and INDPRO growth conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, leading to expectations of higher future short-term rates and a corresponding increase in the 10-year Treasury yield.
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate robust consumer demand and economic health, which can lead to higher inflation expectations and increased borrowing demand, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher in the short term.
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 55% of observations when RSAFS MoM growth exceeds 0.5% over a 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong non-farm payroll employment growth signals a tightening labor market and robust economic activity, which typically leads to higher wage growth and inflation, prompting expectations of higher interest rates and thus pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up.
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing sector and overall economic weakness, leading to lower inflation expectations and a flight to safety, which drives down the 10-year Treasury yield.
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by >0.5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts, especially when coupled with a rising federal funds rate, indicates a significant slowdown in interest-rate sensitive sectors and broader economic weakness, leading to lower growth and inflation expectations, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% MoM AND FEDFUNDS has increased by > 0.25% in the last 3 months
failure_condition: The 10-year Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 50% of periods where HOUST declines >10% MoM and FEDFUNDS has increased >0.25% in the last 3 months.
